tex:
(a) Write $\dfrac{1}{4}$ as a decimal.


    

(b) Write $\dfrac{36}{124}$ as a fraction in its lowest terms.


    

(c) Work out $\dfrac{5}{8}$ of 128.


    

(d) Write down all the factors of 24.


    

(e) Find the highest common factor (HCF) of 24 and 108.


    

(f) Write down an irrational number between 3 and 9.


    

(g) Write down the value of $25^0$.


    

(h) \$8400 is invested for 2 years at a rate of 3.5\% per year compound interest.\\
     Work out the total amount of interest earned by the end of the 2 years.

    \$ 


(i) Without using a calculator, work out $2\dfrac{1}{3} + \dfrac{4}{5}$.\\
     You must show all your working and give your answer as a mixed number in its simplest form.

(a) Emma records the number of letters in each word in a sentence.
    7 \quad 1 \quad 5 \quad 4 \quad 2 \quad 4 \quad 5 \quad 3 \quad 3 \quad 1 \quad 2 \quad 4
    Find
    (i) (b) the median,

        

(c) the mode,

        

(d) the range.

        

(e) Jack records the number of letters in 25 words.
    (i) (f) \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
                \hline
                Number of letters in a word & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 \\ \hline
                Number of words             & 3 & 1 & 5 & 8 & 6 & 2 \\ \hline
            \end{tabular}
        Calculate the mean.

        

(g) Priti picks one of Jack's words at random.\\
        Find the probability that this word has 4 or more letters.

        

(h) The table shows information about the first 90 words in a book.
    (i) (i) Complete the table.
        \begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
                \hline
                Length of word & Frequency & Pie chart angle \\ \hline
                Very short     & 6         & 24$^\circ$      \\ \hline
                Short          & 34        &                 \\ \hline
                Medium         & 41        &                 \\ \hline
                Long           & 9         &                 \\ \hline
            \end{tabular}


(j) Complete the pie chart to show this information.
        %\begin{InsertScreenShot}
            %    
            %\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle A onto triangle B.

    

(b) Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle A onto triangle C.

    

(c) On the grid, draw the image of triangle A after a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} -2 \\ -3 \end{pmatrix}$.


(d) On the grid, draw the image of triangle A after a rotation of 180$^\circ$ about (2, 1).

(a) A plane leaves Karachi at 15 30 to fly to Bangkok.\\
    The distance is 3840 km.\\
    The plane flies at an average speed of 720 km/h.\\
    The local time in Bangkok is 2 hours ahead of the local time in Karachi.\\
    Find the local time in Bangkok when the plane arrives.


    

(b) In Bangkok a watch costs 2610 baht.\\
    The exchange rate is \$1 = 34.8 baht.\\
    Find the cost of the watch in dollars.

    \$ 


(c) The price of another watch increases from \$18 to \$19.17.\\
    Find the percentage increase in the price of this watch.


    

(d) Raoul makes a profit when he sells his watch for \$36.\\
    The ratio of the price Raoul paid for the watch and the profit he makes is \\
     price paid : profit = 7 : 2.\\
    Work out the profit that Raoul makes.

    \$

(a) Simplify.
    $9x - 2y - 5x - y$

    

(b) $P = 4ab + 3b^2$\\
    Work out the value of $a$ when $P = 35$ and $b = 5$.

    

(c) Solve.
    (i) (d) $10x = 5$

        

(e) $7x - 3 = 2x + 11$

        

(f) $3(2x - 1) = 27$

        

(g) Rearrange $T = 5(p + 2)$ to make $p$ the subject.

    

(h) Solve the simultaneous equations.\\
    You must show all your working.
    \begin{align*}
        3x - y &= 22 \\
        x + 2y &= 5
    \end{align*}

(a) The scale drawing shows the positions of town A and town B.\\
    The scale is 1 centimetre represents 12 kilometres.

        \\[0.5cm]
        Scale : 1 cm to 12 km
    (i) (b) Measure the bearing of town B from town A.

        

(c) Find the actual distance from town A to town B.

        

(d) Town C is on a bearing of 064$^\circ$ from town A and 028$^\circ$ from town B.\\
        On the scale drawing, mark the position of town C.


(e) The bearing of town D from town E is 245$^\circ$.\\
    Work out the bearing of town E from town D.

    

(f) The diagram shows three towns, P, Q and R.

    The bearing of town Q from town P is 090$^\circ$.
    (i) (g) Complete the statement.\\
        Town { } is due west of town { }.


(h) PQ and QR are two sides of a regular decagon.\\
        Work out angle PQR.

(a) The diagram shows a shape made from two rectangles.

    (i) (b) Work out the perimeter.

        

(c) Work out the area.

        

(d) The diagram shows a triangle between two parallel lines, AB and CD.

    Find the value of
    (i) (e) $v$,

        

(f) $w$,

        

(g) $y$.

        

(h) These two cuboids have the same volume.

    Find the value of $h$.

    

(i) The diagram shows two similar triangles, ABC and DEF.

    Calculate $DF$.

(a) (i) (b) Complete the table of values for $y = x^2 - 2x$.
        \begin{tabular}{|c||c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
                \hline
                $x$ & $-2$ & $-1$ & $0$ & $1$ & $2$ & $3$ & $4$ \\ \hline
                $y$ & 8    &      & 0   & $-1$ & 0   & 3   & 8   \\ \hline
            \end{tabular}


(c) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^2 - 2x$ for $-2 \le x \le 4$.

        

(d) Here are the first four terms of a sequence.
    3 \qquad 9 \qquad 15 \qquad 21
    (i) (e) Find the next term.

        

(f) Write down the rule for continuing this sequence.

        

(g) Find the $n$th term of this sequence.

$x$$-2$$-1$$0$$1$$2$$3$$4$
$y$80$-1$038

tex:
(a) By rounding each number correct to 1 significant figure, show that an estimate for this calculation is 20.
    $\dfrac{9.78 + 31.562}{0.381 \times 5.09}$

    

(b) Write these numbers in order, smallest first.
    $\dfrac{22}{7} \qquad 3.142 \qquad \dfrac{333}{106} \qquad 3.1416$
    {} < {} < {} < {}


(c) The length, $p$ cm, of a pencil is 9.8 cm, correct to 2 significant figures.\\
    Complete the statement about the value of $p$.
    {} $\le p <$ {}


(d) Calculate $3.142 - \dfrac{52163}{16604}$.\\
    Give your answer in standard form correct to 2 significant figures.
